content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 2, 2002

Nathaniel Bates
1101 Silver Belt Drive
Richmond, CA 94803

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-02-027

Dear Mr. Bates:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please note that the Commission does not provide advice relating to past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) Therefore, any conclusions contained herein apply only to prospective actions.

Additionally, our advice relates only to provisions of the Act. We recommend that you also contact the Attorney General's office for further guidance relating to the possible application of Government Code section 1090.

QUESTION

May you accept employment with a company if you, as a member of the city council, voted to award a contract to the company?

CONCLUSION

No provision of the Act prohibits you from accepting particular employment. However, accepting employment with the company will create an economic interest for you in the company. Consequently, you would not be able to make, participate in making, or influence decisions materially affecting the company.

_______________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-02-027
Page No. 2

FACTS

Based on your correspondence and a phone conversation with Commission
counsel, you are a member of the Richmond City Council. In January 2002, as a council
member, you voted to award a contract to Viron Energy Services, an energy company
doing business in Richmond. In February 2002, Viron offered you employment as a
consultant to assist the company with its contract of other nearby cities. You seek advice
as to whether your acceptance of this employment is permissible under the Act.

ANALYSIS

While no provision of the Act² bars the acceptance by a public official of
particular employment, please be aware that under sections 87103(c) and (d), your
employment with this company will create an economic interest in the company.
Consequently, it is possible that a conflict of interest may arise if this company is later
involved in a decision before the city council.

For each decision that comes before the city council, you must apply the Act's
conflict-of-interest analysis to determine whether you will have a conflict of interest.
This analysis along with other conflict-of-interest rules of the Act are addressed in the
Commission document "Can I Vote? Conflicts of Interest Overview" (enclosed). Please
note that other provisions of law outside of the Act may impact your employment decision.
The Commission's jurisdiction is limited to the Act.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Natalie Bocanegra
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

NB:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\02-027

² Note that section 87407 of the Act provides: "No state administrative official, elected state
officer, or designated employee of the Legislature shall make, participate in making, or use his or her
official position to influence, any governmental decision directly relating to any person with whom he or
she is negotiating, or has any arrangement concerning, prospective employment." Because you are a
member of the city council, a local agency, the rule of section 87407 is not applicable to you.
